273	\subsection{Stage 6 sub-decomposition: 00OE + 02JK chain (iter-198)}
274	\label{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition}
275	
276	Stage 6 is the residual gap inside
277	\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}'s six-stage chain: given Stages
278	1--5b (a standard-smooth presentation of an affine chart
279	\(R \to S\) at a point \(z\), with \(\Omega_{S/R}\) free of rank \(n\)
280	and \(\Omega_{S_\mathfrak q / R}\) free of rank \(n\) on the stalk
281	\(S_\mathfrak q = \mathcal O_{X, z}\)), one must conclude that
282	\(S_\mathfrak q\) is a regular local ring. The Stacks proof of tag
283	\texttt{00TT} ultimately reduces to two ingredients which Mathlib does
284	not yet ship at commit \texttt{b80f227}: (6.A) the smooth-algebra
285	Krull-dimension formula (Stacks tag \texttt{00OE}, in the algebra
286	chapter as Lemma~\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}),
287	and (6.B) the cotangent / K\"ahler-differential bridge over a field
288	base (Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, in the algebra chapter as
289	Lemma~\texttt{lemma-differential-seq} plus the separable-residue-field
290	refinement of Lemma~\texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}). The two
291	ingredients combine (6.C) into the regularity conclusion via
292	\(\dim S_\mathfrak q = \mathrm{rank}_{S_\mathfrak q}\Omega_{S_\mathfrak
293	q / R} = \dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\),
294	which is the cotangent-space characterisation of regularity that
295	Mathlib's \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.iff\_finrank\_cotangentSpace}
296	already exposes. The next three lemmas package the three sub-gaps as
297	named iter-199+ prover targets.
298	
299	\begin{lemma}
300	  [Stage 6.A --- Smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE)]
301	  \label{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
302	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
303	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
304	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field
305	  % (canonically known as tag 00OE; tag-to-label cross-check not in the
306	  % local `tags/tags` file, see iter-198 writer report note) (read from
307	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L28206--28217 in section
308	  % "Dimension theory of finitely generated $k$-algebras").
309	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be a field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$
310	  % algebra. Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak p \subset S$ be a prime
311	  % ideal, and let $x \in X$ be the corresponding point. Then we have
312	  % $\dim_x(X) = \dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) + \text{trdeg}_k\
313	  % \kappa(\mathfrak p)$."
314	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~00OE (\(=\) \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}).}
315	  Let \(k\) be a field, let \(S\) be a finite-type \(k\)-algebra, and let
316	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\) be a prime ideal corresponding to a point
317	  \(x \in \mathrm{Spec}(S)\). Then
318	  \[
319	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)
320	    \;=\; \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q).
321	  \]
322	  In particular, if \(\mathfrak q\) is a maximal ideal (so \(x\) is a
323	  closed point) and \(k\) is algebraically closed, then
324	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = k\) (Nullstellensatz), so
325	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\) and
326	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)\).
327	
328	  \paragraph{Geometric application.} Applied to the iter-193 Stage~3
329	  setup --- \(k = \bar k\), \(S = \Gamma(X.\mathrm{left}, V)\) a
330	  standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\) (Stage
331	  3), and \(\mathfrak q\) the prime defining the point \(z \in V\) --- the
332	  formula yields \(\dim(\mathcal O_{X, z}) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n -
333	  \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(z)\). For \(z\) a closed point this is
334	  simply \(n\); for the generic point of a codim-\(1\) subvariety
335	  (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) it is \(1\).
336	\end{lemma}
337	
338	\begin{lemma}
339	  [Stage 6.B --- Cotangent \(\leftrightarrow\) K\"ahler over a field (Stacks 02JK)]
340	  \label{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
341	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
342	  % \lean{...} updated iter-199 review: iter-199 prover landed the
343	  % closed-point iso as a project-local axiom-clean helper (private
344	  % theorem in AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme namespace). Companion siblings:
345	  %   cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
346	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue
347	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue (closed-point bundled)
348	  \uses{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
349	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-differential-seq (canonically
350	  % known as the right-exact conormal portion of tag 02JK; the
351	  % short-exact / split refinement is the proof step inside
352	  % lemma-separable-smooth) (read from
353	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L34087--34104 and L38724--38766).
354	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In diagram (\ref{equation-functorial-omega}),
355	  % suppose that $S \to S'$ is surjective with kernel $I \subset S$, and
356	  % assume that $R' = R$. Then there is a canonical exact sequence of
357	  % $S'$-modules
358	  % $$I/I^2 \longrightarrow \Omega_{S/R} \otimes_S S' \longrightarrow
359	  % \Omega_{S'/R} \longrightarrow 0$$
360	  % The leftmost map is characterized by the rule that $f \in I$ maps
361	  % to $\text{d}f \otimes 1$."
362	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Hence we get
363	  % $$\dim_{\kappa} \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa = \dim_\kappa
364	  % \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 + \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) \geq \dim R +
365	  % \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) = \dim_{\mathfrak q} S$$
366	  % (see Lemma \ref{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field} for
367	  % the last equality) with equality if and only if $R$ is regular."
368	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~02JK (right-exact half = \texttt{lemma-differential-seq}; separable-residue refinement inside \texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}).}
369	  Let \(k\) be a field and let \(R\) be a Noetherian local
370	  \(k\)-algebra with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and residue field
371	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\). The canonical exact sequence
372	  \[
373	    \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
374	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa
375	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{\kappa / k}
376	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
377	  \]
378	  identifies the cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) (a
379	  \(\kappa\)-vector space) with the kernel of the right map. When
380	  \(\kappa / k\) is separable (in particular when \(k\) is
381	  algebraically closed and \(\kappa = k\), so \(\Omega_{\kappa / k}
382	  = 0\)) the leftmost map is injective and the sequence is short exact
383	  --- in fact split short exact when there is a \(k\)-algebra section
384	  \(\kappa \to R\) (e.g.\ at a closed point of a \(\bar k\)-variety).
385	  In particular, over an algebraically closed field \(k = \bar k\) and
386	  at a closed point \(\mathfrak m\),
387	  \[
388	    \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa \;\simeq\; \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
389	  \]
390	  as \(\kappa\)-vector spaces; hence
391	  \(\dim_\kappa(\Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa)
392	   = \dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\).
393	\end{lemma}

397	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
398	  % NOTE (iter-199): the Lean target for this 6.C assembly is the body
399	  % of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth}

405	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
406	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
407	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
408	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
409	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
410	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
411	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
412	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
413	  regular local ring of Krull dimension
414	  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
415	  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
416	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
417	  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
418	  dimension equals \(1\).
419	\end{lemma}
420	
421	\begin{proof}
422	  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
423	  \begin{enumerate}
424	    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
425	      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)
426	      is free of rank \(n\) over \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
427	    \item By \cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (Stage 6.A,
428	      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals
429	      \(\dim_z \Spec S - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\),
430	      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension
431	      \(n\) equals \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\).
432	      (For the standard-smooth presentation the dimension
433	      \(\dim_z \Spec S = n\) follows from the global-complete-intersection
434	      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see
435	      Stacks \texttt{lemma-relative-global-complete-intersection-smooth}.)
436	    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,
437	      Stacks 02JK) the residue-field tensor \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}
438	      \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \kappa(\mathfrak q)\) is isomorphic to
439	      \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) as \(\kappa(\mathfrak q)\)-vector
440	      spaces. Combined with (1), \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak
441	      m / \mathfrak m^2 = n\) at the closed-point case (and in general
442	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\) by base-change
443	      of the standard-smooth presentation to the closed-point fibre).
444	    \item Compare (2) and (3): both \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) and
445	      \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) equal
446	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the
447	      regularity criterion for Noetherian local rings (the equality
448	      \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2 = \dim R\) is the
449	      defining condition for a regular local ring, Stacks
450	      \texttt{definition-regular-local} \(=\) Mathlib
451	      \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}) then gives \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
452	      regular. The scheme-level transfer
453	      \(S_{\mathfrak q} = \mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Stage 3
454	      \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} on the affine chart) completes
455	      the conclusion.
456	  \end{enumerate}
457	\end{proof}

632	\paragraph{Iter-201+ Jacobian-regular-sequence witness recipe.} The
633	residual substrate of Stage 6.A is the Jacobian-criterion
634	regular-sequence witness for the relations of an
635	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\).
636	
637	\textbf{API choice} (per iter-201 \texttt{progress-critic route201}
638	flag): the project-local target predicate is Mathlib's
639	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular}\), \emph{not}
640	\(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\). Reason: the iter-200 substrate
641	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}\)
642	(\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L807) consumes the strong predicate
643	\(\texttt{IsRegular}\) (via Mathlib's

645	the localised stalk \(R'_{m'}\) is local, so by Mathlib's
646	\(\texttt{IsLocalRing.isRegular\_iff\_isWeaklyRegular\_of\_subset\_maximalIdeal}\)
647	the two predicates are equivalent under the standing hypothesis that
648	the relations lie in the maximal ideal --- which holds because
649	the Jacobian-criterion conclusion is precisely that the relations
650	generate (a subset of) the maximal ideal at the closed point. The
651	recipe builds \(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\) first (it is the
652	naturally-arising predicate from the Koszul-complex argument) and
653	then promotes to \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) via the bridge lemma; the
654	final consumer call uses the \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) form.

669	\(\Rightarrow\) (Matsumura 14.2 / Stacks 00NQ on a regular local

849	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textbf{Definition.} We say a scheme \(X\) is \emph{regular in
850	  % codimension one} (or sometimes \emph{nonsingular in codimension one}) if every

852	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The most important examples of such schemes are nonsingular
853	  % varieties over a field (I, \S5) and noetherian normal schemes. On a nonsingular
854	  % variety the local ring of every closed point is regular (I, 5.1), hence all the

862	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\) and let \(\eta \in X\) be a point with
863	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\). Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a
864	  discrete valuation ring with fraction field the function field \(K(X)\) of \(X\).
865	\end{lemma}

868	
869	  % NOTE (iter-185 writer): the `` marker on this proof block reflects
870	  % the Krull-dim half (Tier-1 axiom-clean closure achieved iter-184 via
871	  % `Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` from the iter-183
872	  % `Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean`, see
873	  % `\cref{chap:Albanese_CoheightBridge}`). The `IsRegularLocalRing` half is
874	  % tracked separately as the Stacks 00TT gap and is now packaged in
875	  % `\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}` above; that block carries no
876	  % `` because the Mathlib hooks (i)--(iv) in its proof sketch are not
877	  % all axiom-clean at commit `b80f227`. The composition (00TT regularity +
878	  % iter-183 Krull-dim bridge) inside the private Lean helper
879	  % `smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`
880	  % (`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean:223`) keeps a single
881	  % typed `sorry` on the regularity half pending an iter-185+ prover round.
882	  By \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} (Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}, the
883	  Jacobian-criterion direction), the stalk \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\) is regular for
884	  every point \(x \in X\) --- in particular at every closed point (the case
885	  Hartshorne~I.5.1 states classically). Localising a regular local ring at a
886	  prime ideal yields a regular local ring; in particular, for \(\eta \in X\) with
887	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\), the local ring
888	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is regular of Krull dimension \(1\). A regular local ring of Krull dimension \(1\) is exactly a
889	  discrete valuation ring (Atiyah--Macdonald, Proposition 9.2; or Stacks tag
890	  \texttt{00PD}). Its fraction field is the function field \(K(X)\) of \(X\) by
891	  irreducibility of \(X\) (the unique generic point of \(X\) lies above \(\eta\), and the
892	  stalk at the generic point of an integral scheme is the function field).
893	
894	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
895	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} is the
896	  instance/lemma stating that \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a Mathlib
897	  \texttt{IsDiscreteValuationRing} under the typeclass set
898	  \texttt{[Smooth\,(\dots)\,X.hom]\ [IsIntegral X.left]} and the hypothesis
899	  \texttt{Order.coheight\ \(\eta\) = 1}. The fraction-field identification with
900	  \texttt{X.functionField} is a downstream named instance
901	  \texttt{Scheme.fractionRing\_localRing\_eq\_functionField} (or whatever Mathlib name
902	  the prover chooses at commit \texttt{b80f227}). The regular-local-ring-of-dimension-1
903	  \(\Leftrightarrow\) DVR equivalence is shipped in Mathlib as
904	  \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.isDiscreteValuationRing\_of\_dim\_one} (or analogous);
905	  the prover re-exports it rather than re-proving.
906	\end{proof}

910	
911	The first input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is the following: a rational map from a normal
912	variety to a \emph{complete} variety automatically extends across every codim-\(1\)
913	point.

923	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
924	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textsc{Theorem 3.1.} \emph{A rational map $\varphi \colon V
925	  % \dashrightarrow W\( from a normal variety \)V\( to a complete variety \)W$ is defined
926	  % on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension
927	  % \(\geq 2\).}"
928	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16.}
929	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a complete variety over
930	  \(\bar k\), and let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. Then the
931	  indeterminacy locus \(Z(f)\) (\cref{def:indeterminacy_locus}) has codimension
932	  \(\geq 2\) in \(X\); equivalently, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
933	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}).
934	
935	  Specialising further: if in addition \(f\) \emph{is} codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
936	  ``for free'' (i.e.\ no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in \(Z(f)\)), then
937	  \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) = X\) and \(f\) extends to a regular morphism $\widetilde f \colon X
938	  \to Y\(. The extension is unique because \)X\( is reduced and \)\mathrm{Dom}(f)$ is dense
939	  in \(X\).
940	\end{theorem}

1255	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
1256	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
1257	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).

1260	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
1261	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
1262	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
1263	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the

1266	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;

1269	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1270	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1271	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1272	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1273	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1274	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1275	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1276	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
1277	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
1278	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
1279	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
1280	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
1281	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
1282	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
1283	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
1284	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
1285	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
1286	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
1287	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
1288	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
1289	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
1290	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
1291	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
1292	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1293	    intro hF
1294	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
1295	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
1296	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
1297	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)

1301	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
1302	
1303	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1304	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1305	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1306	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1307	
1308	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1309	
1310	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1311	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1312	
1313	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1314	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1315	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1316	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1317	
1318	The proof combines two steps:
1319	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1320	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1321	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1322	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1323	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1324	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1325	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1326	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1327	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1328	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1329	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1330	
1331	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1332	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1333	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1334	
1335	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1336	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1337	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1338	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1339	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1340	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1341	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1342	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1343	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1344	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1345	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1346	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1347	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1348	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1349	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1350	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1351	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1352	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1353	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1354	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1355	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1356	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:
1357	  --
1358	  -- * Step 1 (codim-1 components of Z(f) ruled out) uses the valuative
1359	  --   criterion of properness on the complete target Y plus the DVR-stalk
1360	  --   structure at codim-1 points (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` above, gated
1361	  --   on `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6 Mathlib gap).
1362	  -- * Step 2 (codim-≥2 extension) uses the depth-≥2 local-cohomology
1363	  --   vanishing `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point (Stacks 0AVF), a
1364	  --   genuine Mathlib gap at commit `b80f227`. The codim-1-free hypothesis
1365	  --   `_hf` alone suffices for Step 1's conclusion but Step 2 requires the
1366	  --   local-cohomology / `Module.depth` bridge from
1367	  --   `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`).
1368	  --
1369	  -- Once `Dom(f) = ⊤` is established, the unique morphism extension is the
1370	  -- composition `X.left.topIso.inv ≫ (X.left.isoOfEq h).inv ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom`;
1371	  -- uniqueness follows from the reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1372	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).
1373	  --
1374	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: this lane is gated on Stacks 0AVF
1375	  -- (depth-≥2 H¹ vanishing) + `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6.
1376	  sorry

1378	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1379	
1380	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1381	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1382	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1383	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1384	
1385	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1386	
1387	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1388	codim 1.**
1389	
1390	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1391	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1392	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1393	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1394	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1395	
1396	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1397	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1398	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1399	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1400	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1401	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1402	
1403	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1404	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1405	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1406	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1407	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1408	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1409	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1410	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1411	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1412	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1413	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1414	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1415	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1416	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1417	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1418	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1419	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1420	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1421	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1422	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1423	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1424	  --
1425	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1426	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1427	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1428	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1429	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1430	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1431	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1432	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1433	  --   the domain of definition.
1434	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1435	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1436	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1437	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1438	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1439	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1440	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1441	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1442	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's
1443	  --   `RamificationTheory.PrincipalIdeal` provides Krull's HPP for ideals
1444	  --   but the scheme-level codim-1 intersection-with-diagonal lemma is
1445	  --   not packaged.
1446	  --
1447	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: gated on (a) the function-field-pullback bridge for
1448	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1449	  -- (b) the codim-1 pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma (Hartshorne
1450	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form).

1453	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
1454	
1455	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
1456	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
1457	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
1458	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
1459	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
1460	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
1461	witnesses are constructed in this project).
1462	
1463	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
1464	
1465	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
1466	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
1467	
1468	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
1469	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
1470	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
1471	
1472	which requires the pullback machinery
1473	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
1474	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
1475	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
1476	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
1477	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
1478	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
1479	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
1480	
1481	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
1482	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
1483	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
1484	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
1485	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
1486	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
1487	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
1488	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
1489	the pullback machinery lands.
1490	
1491	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
1492	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
1493	
1494	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
1495	
1496	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
1497	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
1498	are equivalent:
1499	
1500	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
1501	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
1502	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
1503	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
1504	
1505	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
1506	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
1507	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
1508	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
1509	construct partial-map representatives).
1510	
1511	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
1512	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
1513	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
1514	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
1515	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
1516	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
1517	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point

964	% has a representative defined on an open subset that meets \(Z\) in a nonempty set,
965	% which is a contradiction."
966	\begin{proof}
967	  
968	  Following Milne's two-step proof.
969	
970	  \emph{Step 1: rule out codim-\(1\) components.} Suppose for contradiction that \(Z(f)\)
971	  contains a prime divisor \(W \subseteq X\) (i.e.\ a codim-\(1\) irreducible component).
972	  Let \(\eta \in W\) be its generic point, so \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\). By
973	  \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a discrete
974	  valuation ring with fraction field the function field \(K(X)\). Any representative
975	  \((U, \varphi_U) \in f\) has \(U \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f) \neq \emptyset\) and so determines
976	  a \(\bar k\)-algebra map from the function field \(K(Y)\) of \(Y\) to \(K(X)\): explicitly,
977	  \(\varphi_U\) defines a morphism of schemes \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X)) \to Y\) (since
978	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X))\) is the generic point of \(X\)). By the valuative criterion
979	  of properness applied to the complete variety \(Y\)
980	  (\emph{cf.}\ Hartshorne~II.4.7, cited verbatim in Milne~3.1's proof), this morphism
981	  extends to a morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}) \to Y\). Standard
982	  algebraic-geometry spreading then exhibits an open neighbourhood \(V\) of \(\eta\) in
983	  \(X\) on which \(f\) has a regular representative; in particular $\eta \in
984	  \mathrm{Dom}(f)\(, contradicting \)\eta \in W \subseteq Z(f)\(. Hence no codim-\)1$
985	  component of \(X\) lies in \(Z(f)\), i.e.\ \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\).
986	
987	  \emph{Step 2: extend across codim-\(\geq 2\) points (the "codim-1-indeterminacy-free
988	  \(\Rightarrow\) extension" half).} Suppose now that \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)
989	  but is non-empty (we want to deduce \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) under the additional input
990	  that ``\(f\) is already codim-1-indeterminacy-free, by hypothesis''). Pick a closed
991	  point \(x \in Z(f)\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{x\}} \geq 2\). By
992	  \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} applied to the regular local ring
993	  \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\), we have
994	  \(\mathrm{depth}(\mathcal O_{X, x}) = \dim(\mathcal O_{X, x}) \geq 2\). The sheaf
995	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on the punctured neighbourhood \(V \smallsetminus \{x\}\)
996	  (any small affine neighbourhood \(V \ni x\)) has \(H^0_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\)
997	  and \(H^1_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\) (the depth-\(\geq 2\) characterisation of
998	  local-cohomology vanishing, Stacks tag \texttt{0AVF}; cf.\ Hartshorne~III.3.5 for
999	  the surface case). Pull back the coordinates of any affine chart of \(Y\) around the
1000	  closure \(\overline{f(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \cap V)}\) along \(f\); the resulting sections of
1001	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on \(V \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) extend uniquely to sections on \(V\),
1002	  yielding a regular representative of \(f\) on \(V\). Hence \(x \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\),
1003	  contradicting \(x \in Z(f)\). Therefore \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) and \(f\) extends to a
1004	  regular morphism \(\widetilde f \colon X \to Y\).
1005	
1006	  Uniqueness of \(\widetilde f\) is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement
1007	  principle: two morphisms \(X \to Y\) that agree on the dense open \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\)
1008	  agree on all of \(X\).
1009	\end{proof}
1010	
1011	\begin{remark}
1012	  \label{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}
1013	  \textbf{Where Auslander--Buchsbaum enters.} Step~1 of the proof uses only normality
1014	  + the valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne~II.4.7) and is characteristic-free.
1015	  Step~2 uses the depth-\(\geq 2\) extension property at a codim-\(\geq 2\) regular
1016	  point, which is exactly what \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} of
1017	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} supplies (regular local of dimension \(d\)
1018	  \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay of depth \(d\)). For our application the source \(X\) is a
1019	  smooth variety, so the regular-local-ring hypothesis is automatic; the Cohen--Macaulay
1020	  consequence is what feeds the local-cohomology vanishing.
1021	
1022	  Concretely, when \(X\) is a smooth surface (e.g.\ \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1\) or
1023	  \(C \times C\) for a smooth projective curve \(C\)), the codim-\(\geq 2\) closed points of
1024	  \(X\) are exactly the closed points of \(X\) (because \(\dim X = 2\)), and at each such
1025	  point \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\) is regular of dimension \(2\), hence Cohen--Macaulay of
1026	  depth \(2\). The smooth-surface case is the one A.4.c will invoke when extending the
1027	  symmetric-product rational map \(C^{(g)} \dashrightarrow A\) along the Abel--Jacobi
1028	  birational equivalence to \(J = \Pic^0_{C/k}\).
1029	\end{remark}

1031	\section{Milne Lemma 3.3: indeterminacy locus of a map to a group variety is codim 1}
1032	\label{sec:milne_lem33}
1033	
1034	The second input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is structural: when the target \(Y\) is a group
1035	variety, the indeterminacy locus is either empty or of \emph{pure} codimension \(1\).
1036	Combined with \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s ``codimension \(\geq 2\)'' conclusion,
1037	this forces the locus to be empty.
1038	
1039	\begin{lemma}
1040	
1041	\leanok
1042	  [Milne Lemma~3.3: pure-codim-\(1\) structure of the indeterminacy locus into a group
1043	  variety]
1044	  \label{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}
1045	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme}
1046	  
1047	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17
1048	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 23).
1049	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textsc{Lemma 3.3.} \emph{Let \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow G\)
1050	  % be a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety. Then either
1051	  % \(\varphi\) is defined on all of \(V\) or the points where it is not defined form a
1052	  % closed subset of pure codimension 1 in \(V\) (i.e., a finite union of prime
1053	  % divisors).}"
1054	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17.}
1055	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(G\) be a group variety over
1056	  \(\bar k\) (a smooth group scheme of finite type), and let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow
1057	  G\( be a rational map. Then either \)f\( is defined on all of \)X$, or the indeterminacy
1058	  locus \(Z(f) \subseteq X\) is a closed subset of pure codimension \(1\) (i.e.\ a finite
1059	  union of prime divisors).
1060	
1061	  Equivalently: it is \emph{never} the case that \(f\) has a non-empty indeterminacy
1062	  locus all of whose irreducible components have codimension \(\geq 2\).
1063	\end{lemma}
1064

1067	%   \varphi(y)^{-1}$.
1068	% More precisely, if \((U, \varphi_U)\) represents \(\varphi\), then \(\Phi\) is the rational
1069	% map represented by
1070	%   $U \times U \xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U} G \times G
1071	%   \xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}} G
1072	%   \xrightarrow{m} G$.

1075	% it is defined on an open neighbourhood of \((x, x)\); in particular, there will be an
1076	% open subset \(U\) of \(V\) such that \(\Phi\) is defined on \(\{x\} \times U\). After

1095	% not defined at the points \((y, y) \in \Delta \cap \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\).

1099	\begin{proof}
1100	  
1101	  Following Milne's proof of Lemma~3.3. The argument has four sub-steps. Throughout we
1102	  fix a representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\).
1103	
1104	  \emph{Sub-step 1: the difference rational map.} Define
1105	  \[
1106	    \Phi \colon X \times X \dashrightarrow G,
1107	    \qquad
1108	    \Phi(x, y) \;:=\; \varphi(x) \cdot \varphi(y)^{-1},
1109	  \]
1110	  via the composition
1111	  \[
1112	    U \times U
1113	    \;\xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U}\; G \times G
1114	    \;\xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}}\; G \times G
1115	    \;\xrightarrow{m}\; G,
1116	  \]
1117	  where \(\mathrm{inv} \colon G \to G\) is the group-variety inverse and
1118	  \(m \colon G \times G \to G\) is the group law. The map \(\Phi\) is rational by
1119	  composition: its domain is at least \(U \times U \subseteq X \times X\), which is dense
1120	  because \(U\) is dense in \(X\).
1121	
1122	  \emph{Sub-step 2: \(\Phi\)-defined at \((x, x)\) \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\varphi\)-defined at
1123	  \(x\).} If \(\varphi\) is defined at \(x\), the explicit formula \(\Phi(x, x) = e\) (the
1124	  identity of \(G\)) shows \(\Phi\) is defined at \((x, x)\). Conversely, if \(\Phi\) is
1125	  defined at \((x, x)\), openness of \(\mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)\) in \(X \times X\) produces an
1126	  open set \(W \ni (x, x)\) on which \(\Phi\) is defined; by projection (taking the slice
1127	  \(\{x\} \times W' \subseteq W\) for some open \(W' \subseteq X\), possibly shrinking
1128	  \(W'\) so \(\varphi\) is defined on \(W'\) as well --- \(W'\) need not contain \(x\)), the
1129	  identity
1130	  \[
1131	    \varphi(x) \;=\; \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u),
1132	    \qquad u \in W',
1133	  \]
1134	  exhibits a regular representative of \(\varphi\) at \(x\). Hence $x \in
1135	  \mathrm{Dom}(\varphi)\( if and only if \)(x, x) \in \mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)$.
1136	
1137	  \emph{Sub-step 3: rephrase via pullback of local rings.} The rational map \(\Phi\)
1138	  pulls \(\mathcal O_{G, e}\) back to a \(\bar k\)-subalgebra of the function field
1139	  \(K(X \times X)\):
1140	  \[
1141	    \Phi^\ast \colon \mathcal O_{G, e} \;\longrightarrow\; K(X \times X).
1142	  \]
1143	  Because \(\Phi\) sends \((x, x)\) to \(e\) wherever it is defined, \(\Phi\) is defined at
1144	  \((x, x)\) if and only if $\Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e}) \subseteq \mathcal O_{X
1145	  \times X, (x, x)}\( (i.e.\ every local function on \)G\( near \)e$ has no pole at
1146	  \((x, x)\) after pullback).
1147	
1148	  \emph{Sub-step 4: pole locus is pure codimension \(1\).} The variety \(X \times X\) is
1149	  nonsingular (smoothness is stable under products over \(\bar k\)), so the Weil-divisor
1150	  apparatus of \cref{def:codim1_cycles} and the order map of
1151	  \cref{def:order_at_point} apply on \(X \times X\). For any nonzero rational function
1152	  \(f \in K(X \times X)^\ast\) decompose its divisor as
1153	  \[
1154	    \mathrm{div}(f) \;=\; \mathrm{div}(f)_0 - \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty,
1155	  \]
1156	  with \(\mathrm{div}(f)_0\) and \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) effective and
1157	  \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = \mathrm{div}(f^{-1})_0\) (Milne's notation, matching the
1158	  project's \(\div(f) = \sum \ord_Y(f) \cdot Y\) of
1159	  \cref{def:principal_divisor}: \(\mathrm{div}(f)_0\) collects the prime divisors with
1160	  \(\ord_Y(f) > 0\) and \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) those with \(\ord_Y(f) < 0\)). The local
1161	  ring at \((x, x)\) can be characterised as
1162	  \[
1163	    \mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}
1164	    \;=\; \bigl\{\, f \in K(X \times X) \;\big|\;
1165	    \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x) \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
1166	  \]
1167	  Suppose \(f \in K(X \times X)^\ast\) is not in \(\mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}\);
1168	  equivalently, \((x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty)\). The pole divisor
1169	  \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) is a finite sum of prime divisors of \(X \times X\) (i.e.\ a
1170	  closed subset of pure codimension \(1\) in \(X \times X\)). Its intersection with the
1171	  diagonal \(\Delta_X \subseteq X \times X\) is a closed subset of pure codimension \(1\)
1172	  in \(\Delta_X\) (this is Milne's reference to AG~9.2, the standard fact that a prime
1173	  divisor of \(X \times X\) either contains \(\Delta_X\) or meets it in pure codimension
1174	  \(1\); on a nonsingular variety this is an instance of Krull's principal-ideal theorem
1175	  applied to the local equation of \(\Delta_X\) inside \(X \times X\)).
1176	
1177	  \emph{Conclusion.} Suppose \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow G\) is not defined on all of
1178	  \(X\). Pick \(x \in Z(f)\). By Sub-steps~2--3, there is some $f_0 \in
1179	  \Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e})\( with \)(x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty)$.
1180	  By Sub-step~4 the intersection $\mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty) \cap
1181	  \Delta_X\( is pure codimension \)1\( in \)\Delta_X\(, passing through \)(x, x)$.
1182	  Identifying \(\Delta_X\) with \(X\) via the diagonal isomorphism, the corresponding
1183	  closed subset of \(X\) has pure codimension \(1\) and passes through \(x\). By Sub-step~2,
1184	  every point of this codim-\(1\) subset is a point where \(\Phi\) is undefined on the
1185	  diagonal, hence where \(\varphi\) is undefined. Thus \(Z(\varphi)\) contains a pure
1186	  codim-\(1\) closed subset passing through \(x\); since \(x \in Z(\varphi)\) was arbitrary,
1187	  every irreducible component of \(Z(\varphi)\) has codimension exactly \(1\), i.e.\
1188	  \(Z(\varphi)\) is of pure codimension \(1\).
1189	\end{proof}

1191	\begin{remark}
1192	  \label{rmk:milne_lem33_charfree}
1193	  \textbf{Characteristic-free.} The above proof uses only: (i) the group law on \(G\)
1194	  and its inverse (formal smoothness of \(G\) as a group object); (ii) the Weil-divisor
1195	  apparatus on the nonsingular variety \(X \times X\) (Hartshorne~II.6, or
1196	  \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor} in the project's notation); (iii) the
1197	  decomposition of a divisor on a nonsingular variety into effective pieces, and
1198	  Krull's principal-ideal theorem on \(X \times X\). None of these has a characteristic
1199	  restriction, so \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} holds over every
1200	  algebraically closed base field, in arbitrary characteristic. (Milne states the
1201	  lemma in this generality at \S I.3, p.~17.)
1202	\end{remark}

1204	\section{Weil-divisor obstruction to codim-1 extension}
1205	\label{sec:weil_obstruction}
1206	
1207	The proof of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s Step~1 (codim-\(1\) ruling-out) goes
1208	through the valuative criterion. In the project's Weil-divisor language
1209	(\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}) the same obstruction has a cleaner phrasing:
1210	the extension fails at a prime divisor \(W\) if and only if some affine coordinate of
1211	\(f\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\) has a strictly negative order
1212	\(\ord_W(\cdot) < 0\). This is the form the Lean prover needs when consuming
1213	\cref{def:order_at_point} and \cref{def:principal_divisor}.
1214	
1215	\begin{theorem}
1216	
1217	  [Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction]
1218	  \label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}

1253	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If \(Y\) is a prime divisor on \(X\), let \(\eta \in Y\) be its generic
1254	  % point. Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{\eta, X}\) of \(X\) at \(\eta\) is a discrete
1255	  % valuation ring with quotient field \(K\), the function field of \(X\). We call the
1256	  % corresponding discrete valuation \(v_Y\) the \emph{valuation of \(Y\)}."
1257	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let \(f \in K^\ast\) be any nonzero rational function on \(X\).

1260	  % order \(-v_Y(f)\)."
1261	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation \(v_Y\) and the order map).}
1262	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a variety over \(\bar k\),
1263	  let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map, and let \(W \subseteq X\) be a
1264	  prime divisor with generic point \(\eta_W \in W\) (so
1265	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta_W\}} = 1\)). Let \((U, \varphi_U)\) be any representative of
1266	  \(f\) with \(U \cap (X \smallsetminus W) \neq \emptyset\). The following are equivalent:
1267	  \begin{enumerate}
1268	    \item \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) (i.e.\ \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\), equivalently
1269	      \(W \not\subseteq Z(f)\)).
1270	    \item For some (equivalently, every) affine open neighbourhood \(V \subseteq Y\) of
1271	      the generic image of \(f\) near \(W\), every regular function $g \in
1272	      \mathcal O_Y(V)\( pulls back to a rational function \)\varphi_U^\ast(g) \in K(X)$
1273	      satisfying
1274	      \[
1275	        \ord_W\bigl(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\bigr) \;\geq\; 0.
1276	      \]
1277	  \end{enumerate}
1278	
1279	  Consequently: \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
1280	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}) if and only if, for every prime divisor
1281	  \(W \subseteq X\) and every affine open \(V \subseteq Y\) as above, the pullback
1282	  \(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\) has \(\ord_W \geq 0\) for all \(g \in \mathcal O_Y(V)\).
1283	\end{theorem}
1284	
1285	\begin{proof}
1286	  
1287	  By \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\) is a
1288	  DVR with fraction field \(K(X)\) and uniformiser determined by \(W\); by
1289	  \cref{def:order_at_point} its valuation is exactly \(\ord_W\). The local ring at
1290	  \(\eta_W\) is therefore characterised inside \(K(X)\) as the subring
1291	  \[
1292	    \mathcal O_{X, \eta_W} \;=\; \bigl\{\, h \in K(X) \;\big|\; \ord_W(h) \geq 0
1293	    \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
1294	  \]
1295	  The rational map \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) if and only if, for any affine open
1296	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq Y\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\),
1297	  the pullback \(\varphi_U^\ast \colon A \to K(X)\) lands in
1298	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\): this is the standard local characterisation of where a
1299	  rational map is regular at a codim-\(1\) point of a smooth variety (it is the
1300	  ``regular = no pole'' criterion). By the displayed identity, the latter is
1301	  equivalent to \(\ord_W(\varphi_U^\ast(g)) \geq 0\) for every \(g \in A\), which is
1302	  condition (2). The equivalence (1)~\(\Leftrightarrow\)~(2) is therefore established.
1303	
1304	  For the consequence, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free iff no prime divisor \(W\) is
1305	  contained in \(Z(f)\), which by the equivalence above is iff the \(\ord_W \geq 0\)
1306	  condition holds for every prime divisor \(W\).
1307	\end{proof}
1308	
1309	\begin{remark}
1310	  \label{rmk:weil_obstruction_application}
1311	  \textbf{Why this form is the prover-facing one.} In the consumer chapter
1312	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} the Lean prover combines
1313	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} with \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}; in
1314	  isolation, the abstract codim-\(\geq 2\) statement
1315	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} suffices and \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
1316	  is not invoked. The Weil-divisor form is the bridge to the project's
1317	  \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API, which is the form A.4.a's own internal sub-proofs
1318	  use when establishing \emph{specific} extensions in genus-\(0\) classifying-map
1319	  arguments and in the symmetric-product Albanese chapter
1320	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}.
1321	\end{remark}

1323	\begin{lemma}
1324	
1325	\leanok
1326	  [Definedness at a prime divisor, repackaged as a through-the-point partial map]
1327	  \label{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle}
1328	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point}
1329	  Let \(X\) and \(Y\) be varieties over \(\bar k\) and let
1330	  \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. For a prime divisor
1331	  \(W \subseteq X\) with generic point \(\eta_W = W.\mathrm{point}\), the
1332	  following are equivalent:
1333	  \begin{enumerate}
1334	    \item \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) (\(f\) is defined at the generic point of
1335	      \(W\)).
1336	    \item There exists a partial-map representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\) ---
1337	      i.e.\ a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) on a dense open
1338	      \(U \subseteq X\) whose induced rational map equals \(f\) --- whose domain
1339	      \(U\) contains \(\eta_W\).
1340	  \end{enumerate}
1341	\end{lemma}
1342	
1343	\begin{proof}
1344	
1345	  The lemma is a definitional reshuffle of the Mathlib membership predicate
1346	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.mem\_domain}, which already states that
1347	  \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) iff there is some partial-map representative of
1348	  \(f\) defined at \(\eta_W\); the only content here is reordering the two
1349	  conjuncts under the existential so the \(\varphi_U \mapsto f\) component lands
1350	  first (matching the way later witnesses in the project construct partial-map
1351	  representatives). The forward and backward implications are immediate from
1352	  the unfolded definition.
1353	\end{proof}

1483	\section{Out of scope}
1484	\label{sec:codim1_out_of_scope}
1485	
1486	This chapter packages \emph{only} the two extension inputs and the Weil-divisor
1487	reformulation needed for A.4.a. The following adjacent content is explicitly out of
1488	scope:
1489	\begin{itemize}
1490	  \item \textbf{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular \(\Rightarrow\)
1491	    Cohen--Macaulay corollary.} Proved in \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}
1492	    as \cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} and \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}; this
1493	    chapter only consumes them (in Step~2 of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}; see
1494	    \cref{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}).
1495	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.2 itself.} The combination of
1496	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} into the
1497	    statement ``a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety
1498	    extends'' lives in \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} as
1499	    \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends}; this chapter only supplies the two inputs.
1500	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.4 / Corollary~3.7 / Theorem~3.8 / Proposition~3.9 /
1501	    Proposition~3.10.} The remainder of Milne~\S I.3 (the ``map between AVs is
1502	    translated homomorphism'' family of results, and the
1503	    \(\mathbb A^1 \dashrightarrow A\) / unirational\(\dashrightarrow A\) constancy
1504	    statements) is downstream of \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends} but is not
1505	    needed by A.4 in the project's current routing: the genus-\(0\) base case is
1506	    handled via the proven Rigidity Lemma + \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut
1507	    (\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}), and the positive-genus arm consumes only the bare
1508	    Theorem~3.2 statement.
1509	  \item \textbf{Weil-divisor degree on \(X \times X\) for \(X\) a curve.} The proof of
1510	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} invokes the Weil-divisor apparatus on the
1511	    smooth surface \(X \times X\) but does \emph{not} need the degree map of
1512	    \cref{def:divisor_degree} (which is curve-specific); only the codim-\(1\)
1513	    decomposition \(\div(f) = \div(f)_0 - \div(f)_\infty\) on a nonsingular surface is
1514	    used. The bilinear degree formula on \(X \times X\) for \(X\) a curve (intersection
1515	    numbers, Hartshorne~V.1) is out of scope.
1516	  \item \textbf{Cartier divisors and the Cartier/Weil comparison.} On a smooth variety
1517	    they coincide and the chapter uses Weil throughout; the Cartier formalism is not
1518	    introduced.
1519	  \item \textbf{Generalisation to non-algebraically-closed base fields.} The
1520	    project's positive-genus arm runs over \(\bar k\) throughout; descent of the
1521	    extension to a non-algebraically-closed base, if ever needed, would require
1522	    Galois-descent machinery and is not in scope.
1523	  \item \textbf{Resolution of indeterminacy by blow-ups} (Hironaka-style, for higher
1524	    indeterminacy codimensions on a smooth variety with a non-group-variety target):
1525	    out of scope. The combination of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and
1526	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} for \(G\) a group variety is sharper than any
1527	    blow-up-based approach because it produces an extension on the original \(X\)
1528	    rather than on a blow-up; no blow-up apparatus is needed.
1529	\end{itemize}

60	    {ix sx : Type*} [Finite sx]
61	    (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx)
62	    (p : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime] :
63	    LinearIndependent (Localization.AtPrime p)
64	      (fun i : sx =>
65	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent)
66	          (Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
67	            (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)))
68	```

71	`P.toExtension.Cotangent` at any prime `p` of `S`. The resulting family in

73	`Localization.AtPrime p`-linearly independent. Realises Analogue D

76	`Rₛ`-lin-indep over the localised base) of the `coe-stacks00sw` analogist
